Class Grid

java.lang.Object
Grid
public class Grid extends Object
Represents the game board: size, obstacle (rock) positions, and goal cell.

The grid is immutable in shape and goal—rows, columns, and goal are set at construction. Rocks are added after construction via addRock(int, int). The controller and UI use this class to check whether a position is inside the grid, has a rock, or is the goal, so all board rules live in one place.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Grid(int rows, int columns, Position goal)
    Creates a grid with the given dimensions and goal position.

    Method
    Description
    void

    Marks a cell as blocked by a rock.

    Returns the number of columns in the grid.

    getGoal()
    Returns the goal position (charging station).
    int
    getRows()
    Returns the number of rows in the grid.
    boolean
    hasRock(Position position)
    Returns whether the given cell contains a rock (blocked).
    boolean
    isGoal(Position position)
    Returns whether the given position is the goal (charging station) cell.
    boolean
    isInside(Position position)
    Checks whether the given position is within the grid bounds (0 to rows-1, 0 to columns-1).
    String
    render(Robot robot)
    Builds a text representation of the grid with row/column headers and one character per cell (robot, goal, rock, or empty).

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Grid

      public Grid(int rows, int columns, Position goal)
      Creates a grid with the given dimensions and goal position. The goal must be inside the grid. Rocks are not placed here; they are added later with addRock(int, int).
      Parameters:
      rows - number of rows (must be positive)
      columns - number of columns (must be positive)
      goal - the charging-station cell the robot must reach
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if rows or columns are non-positive, or goal is outside the grid

  • Method Details

    • addRock

      public void addRock(int row, int column)
      Marks a cell as blocked by a rock. The cell must be inside the grid and must not be the goal.
      Parameters:
      row - row index of the rock
      column - column index of the rock
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the position is outside the grid or is the goal

    • getRows

      public int getRows()
      Returns the number of rows in the grid.
      Returns:
      row count

    • getColumns

      public int getColumns()
      Returns the number of columns in the grid.
      Returns:
      column count

    • isInside

      public boolean isInside(Position position)
      Checks whether the given position is within the grid bounds (0 to rows-1, 0 to columns-1). Used to prevent the robot from moving off the board.
      Parameters:
      position - the position to check
      Returns:
      true if the position is inside the grid

    • hasRock

      public boolean hasRock(Position position)
      Returns whether the given cell contains a rock (blocked). Returns false if the position is outside the grid.
      Parameters:
      position - the cell to check
      Returns:
      true if that cell has a rock

    • isGoal

      public boolean isGoal(Position position)
      Returns whether the given position is the goal (charging station) cell.
      Parameters:
      position - the position to check
      Returns:
      true if this is the goal cell

    • getGoal

      public Position getGoal()
      Returns the goal position (charging station).
      Returns:
      the goal cell

    • render

      public String render(Robot robot)
      Builds a text representation of the grid with row/column headers and one character per cell (robot, goal, rock, or empty). Used for the plain-console version or debugging.
      Parameters:
      robot - the robot to draw on the grid (its position and direction affect the output)
      Returns:
      a multi-line string showing the board
